topic: §2b.1 stratification paragraph — verification of c3 holes 0+1 patches (tighten 'most' to '11 of these 12', name WIN-061 as single empirical case)
change_summary: Two c3 recommendations integrated: (1) replaced 'most of these contradictions are derivational' with '11 of these 12 contradictions are derivational'; (2) replaced 'the small empirical-prediction subset' with 'the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061, Schumann storm suppression)'. Both patches verified landed.
our_argument_summary: Part 2b §2b.1 verdict-stratification paragraph now reads precise per-WIN counts (11 of 12 derivational + 1 empirical, named) rather than soft quantifiers ('most', 'the small subset'). Sharpens auditability against EXP-489's per_win_categorization without changing argument structure or verdict.

c3_hole0_most_to_11_of_12.current_text: 11 of these 12 contradictions are derivational (incompatible geometry, formula identity with standard physics, or missing mechanism) and visible from the dome's own derivation pages without live data
c3_hole0_most_to_11_of_12.pre_patch_text: most of these contradictions are derivational
c3_hole0_most_to_11_of_12.check: grep: '11 of these 12' present (1×), 'most of these' absent — clean substitution

c3_hole1_WIN061_named.current_text: only the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061, Schumann storm suppression) would actually be exposed by monitoring
c3_hole1_WIN061_named.pre_patch_text: the small empirical-prediction subset would actually be exposed by monitoring
c3_hole1_WIN061_named.check: grep: 'WIN-061, Schumann storm suppression' present in this paragraph — clean substitution; 'small empirical-prediction subset' absent

holes_found.description: Restated carry-over from c3 hole 2 (optional WIN-061 mixed-character precision). C3's hole 2 noted that EXP-489 classifies WIN-061 as 'observational' but wins.json's verdict text actually records two layers — a derivational layer (~22 Hz dome cavity formula vs 7.83 Hz observed Schumann) and a properly observational layer (≥30%-suppression / 6-hour-window threshold has no derivation, needs G3-storm data). The new prose framing 'only the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061)... would actually be exposed by monitoring' implies WIN-061 is purely empirical, understating the derivational refutation. C3 itself marked this 'Not blocking'. The current patch landed without addressing it — that's fine if decider intentionally deferred, but the audit gap persists: a careful reader who clicks through to WIN-061's panel sees a derivational mismatch (formula identity) that the §2b.1 sentence does not advertise.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional, low-priority. Either (a) add a half-sentence as c3 suggested ('only the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061, where the dome's published ≥30% suppression threshold has no derivation and must be checked against real storm data) would actually be exposed by monitoring beyond what the dome's own derivation pages already refute'), or (b) leave as-is and rely on WIN-061's panel for the full picture. Either is defensible; not blocking queue closure.

holes_found.description: Restated carry-over from c3 hole 3 (decider push-metadata defect on queue item 432). C3 flagged that queue 432's context_hints referenced EXP-516 instead of the actual EXP-489 source for that patch. This queue item (436) has context_hints.source_file='monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/SEC-part2b-stratification-paragraph.c3.json' (correct self-reference) and related_issues=['ISS-2318'] (which corresponds to the c1-era issue closed by EXP-490, NOT to c3 holes 0+1 — those don't have a dedicated ISS because they were small enough for direct self-apply). So queue 436's metadata is again loosely attributed. This is the same template carry-over defect c3 hole 3 raised. Not a content defect.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider follow-up: when generating context_hints for a c-N patch-verification push, the related_issues field should point to the ISS(s) created from the c-(N-1) review's holes (or null if those holes were self-applied without ISS), not to an upstream issue from a prior cycle. ISS-2318 was the c1-era issue closed by EXP-490 in the prior decider run; it does not describe the work being verified here.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c3 hole 2 (WIN-061 mixed character) — no ISS exists; marked 'not blocking' by c3; carried as the first hole in this c4 review for visibility
c3 hole 3 (decider context_hints carry-over defect) — no ISS exists; pure decider procedural; carried as the second hole here for visibility

kernel_of_truth.description: The patches' arithmetic precision is genuinely better than the soft quantifiers they replace. '11 of these 12' is auditable against the listed WIN IDs immediately above the sentence; 'most' was not. Naming WIN-061 explicitly closes the audit gap c3 hole 1 identified — a reader can match the empirical-prediction case to the inline ID list without leaving the page (data/sections.json:part2b.html stratification paragraph; EXP-489 per_win_categorization in monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-489-ISS-2317-sc-monitoring-overclaim.json).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patches are correct within the c3 hole 0+1 scope but leave c3 holes 2 and 3 unaddressed. Hole 2 is a genuine residual nuance — WIN-061 IS mixed (derivational + observational), and the current prose reads as if it's purely observational. A pedantic defender could quote the §2b.1 sentence back at us and say 'you concede only WIN-061 needs live data — but your own WIN-061 panel says the formula contradiction (~22 Hz vs 7.83 Hz) is already a no-data refutation, so you should have said 0 of these 12 contradictions need monitoring.' That defender is right on the narrow logic, and the simplest answer is the c3-hole-2 half-sentence addition (data/sections.json:part2b.html stratification paragraph; wins.json:WIN-061 evidence text).

citations_verified: data/sections.json:part2b.html stratification paragraph contains '11 of these 12 contradictions are derivational'
data/sections.json:part2b.html stratification paragraph contains 'single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061, Schumann storm suppression)'

summary_for_decider: Queue 436 (SEC-part2b-stratification-paragraph, class=verification, c4): Both targeted patches verified landed cleanly. (a) 'most of these contradictions are derivational' → '11 of these 12 contradictions are derivational' ✓. (b) 'the small empirical-prediction subset' → 'the single empirical-prediction case (WIN-061, Schumann storm suppression)' ✓. Two minor carry-over holes from c3 remain by design: hole 2 (WIN-061 mixed derivational+observational character precision — optional, not blocking) and hole 3 (decider context_hints metadata defect — same defect recurs in queue 436's own context_hints, related_issues=['ISS-2318'] points to a prior-cycle issue rather than to c3 holes 0+1). No new content defects.
